description: A gardener may dedicate a season's work to a community orchard; a practitioner may dedicate themself to a discipline. 'menai' sets work, effort, or self apart for a person or purpose, either in one act or through continued devotion. 'seru' (commit) binds someone to a course, while dedication also names what or whom that course is for.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm', let air pass through the nose, and release into true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Press the tongue tip behind the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and open into stressed central unrounded 'a'. Raise the tongue high and forward for a separate unrounded 'i'. Keep the 'a.i' hiatus audible.
usage_notes: The work, effort, or self being dedicated appears before 'menai'. The preposition 'wei' introduces the recipient or purpose; reflexive 'miso' makes dedication of oneself explicit. Used as a noun, 'menai' is dedication or an act of dedicating.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Dedication gives a repeated practice a stated purpose. It can be renewed without claiming that every imperfect session has betrayed it.

semantic_domains.activity: setting work, effort, or self apart for a person or purpose
semantic_domains.ritual: dedication expressed through a repeated or ceremonial practice
